name: Cross-audience foundations
when_to_use: Diagnose systemic documentation drift, versioning gaps, ownership failures, or missing telemetry behind a concrete docs failure

name: Developer docs site
when_to_use: Developers fail a quickstart, cannot find reference, or misunderstand upgrade docs

name: README front door
when_to_use: Evaluators bounce off the README or ask support what the project does

name: Changelog and release notes
when_to_use: Users miss breaking changes or cannot tell what a release means for them

name: Contributor onboarding docs
when_to_use: New contributors stall on or misread the onboarding doc

name: Examples and samples
when_to_use: Developers copy stale or broken samples

name: End-user help
when_to_use: Users abandon onboarding, miss hidden help, open tickets after articles, or cannot recover from errors

name: API and tool contracts
when_to_use: Agents or developers call the wrong endpoint/tool, misread parameters, retry unsafely, or branch on unstable errors

name: Audience conflicts
when_to_use: A fix for one audience appears to harm another, such as tooltip-only guidance or prose too vague for agents
